steps.title: Use the unequal correlations
steps.content: The graph shows different model-to-participant correlations for P3's impressionist and color-field painting ratings. If P3 gave equal actual ratings to both styles, the unequal correlations suggest the model's predicted ratings did not treat the two styles the same.

steps.title: Avoid actual-pleasure claims
steps.content: Choice B says the two predicted ratings would be equal, which conflicts with the unequal correlations. Choice C and Choice D make claims about which style P3 would enjoy more, but the graph gives correlations, not direct pleasure rankings. The supported statement is that the predicted ratings differed. The answer is A.
